questions.question_text: How many clock hours of pre-licensing education are required for a West Virginia salesperson license?
questions.explanation: West Virginia requires 90 clock hours (equivalent to 6 college credit hours) of pre-licensing education in commission-approved courses.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed pre-licensing education 6 years ago and now wants to apply for a WV license. Can they use this education?
distractors: Yes, if they pass the exam
questions.explanation: Pre-licensing education must have been completed within 5 years preceding the application date. Education completed 6 years ago would no longer qualify.

questions.question_text: What minimum score must a candidate achieve in each subject area of the WV pre-licensing course?
questions.explanation: Candidates must score 70% or higher in each subject area of the pre-licensing course, which matches the exam passing score of 70% on each section.

questions.question_text: The 90 hours of WV pre-licensing education are equivalent to how many college credit hours?
questions.explanation: The 90 clock hours of pre-licensing education for a WV salesperson license are equivalent to 6 college credit hours. Broker education requires 180 hours (12 credits).

questions.question_text: A student completed a pre-licensing course at a school not approved by the WVREC. Can they use this education to apply?
distractors: Yes, if the course covered 90 hours
No, but they can petition for an exception
questions.explanation: Pre-licensing courses must be approved by the WVREC. Courses from non-approved providers do not satisfy the education requirement regardless of content or hours.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to apply for a West Virginia real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Applicants must be at least 18 years old to apply for a WV salesperson license. They must also have a high school diploma or GED equivalent.

questions.question_text: What education credential must a WV license applicant possess in addition to pre-licensing courses?
questions.explanation: Applicants must have a high school diploma or equivalent (GED) in addition to completing the 90 hours of pre-licensing education.

questions.question_text: WV license applicants must submit fingerprints through which vendor for background checks?
questions.explanation: Fingerprints must be submitted to IdentoGO, the state's designated vendor, for FBI and state criminal history background checks. The processing fee is $45.75.

questions.question_text: A 17-year-old has completed all 90 hours of pre-licensing education and passed the exam. Can they obtain a WV license?
questions.correct_answer: No, applicants must be at least 18
distractors: Yes, passing the exam overrides age rules
Yes, as an apprentice license only
questions.explanation: The minimum age for a WV real estate license is 18 years old, regardless of education completion or exam results. There is no apprentice or minor exception.

questions.question_text: What type of background check is required for WV real estate license applicants?
questions.correct_answer: FBI and state criminal history check
distractors: Credit check and civil court review
questions.explanation: WV requires both FBI and state criminal history background checks via fingerprints submitted to IdentoGO. This is more comprehensive than just a state-level check.

questions.question_text: What passing score is required on each section of the WV real estate licensing exam?
questions.explanation: Candidates must score 70% or higher on both the national and state sections of the exam. The sections are scored independently, and both must be passed.

questions.question_text: The WV salesperson licensing exam is administered by which testing provider?
questions.explanation: West Virginia uses Pearson VUE as its exam provider. The exam is taken electronically at Pearson VUE test sites with immediate scoring.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the national section with 75% but scores 68% on the state section. What is the result?
questions.correct_answer: Fail — must retake the state section
distractors: Pass — national score compensates for state
questions.explanation: West Virginia uses a split exam format where each section is scored independently. A candidate must score 70% on BOTH sections. The 68% on the state section means that section must be retaken.

questions.question_text: How many scored items are on the state portion of the WV salesperson exam?
questions.explanation: The WV salesperson state exam has 50 scored items plus 5-10 pretest items. The national portion has 80 scored items. Total exam time is 4 hours.

questions.question_text: When does a candidate receive their WV licensing exam results?
distractors: At the next WVREC commission meeting
questions.explanation: The WV licensing exam is administered electronically at Pearson VUE test sites with immediate electronic scoring. Candidates know their results right away.
